Dashboard Archive: 2026-03-26 Home
About This Dashboard
What It Does
Short-term momentum ranker scoring 700+ tickers on returns (1d to 1y), SMA gate (above 30/50/100/200 day), ratio consistency across timeframes, and resilience on bad SPY days. Higher composite scores indicate stronger, more consistent short-term momentum.
Today's Reading
# Market Analysis: Momentum Ranker Results Today's data reveals two distinctly different momentum profiles at the top of the rankings. PARR dominates with a score of 94.2 and rank 1, driven by exceptional long-term returns (353% over 1 year) paired with strong consistency—it passes the SMA gates across all four timeframes and maintains a clean momentum signal of 1.26. However, PARR shows notable ratio volatility, with the 1-month-to-1-week ratio spiking to 7.48 before cooling to 1.23 over 3 months, suggesting earlier acceleration that has since moderated. In contrast, CLMT (rank 2) displays a more erratic pattern with a severe dip in the 1-month return (2.53%) followed by explosive 3-month growth (51.7%), indicated by a striking ratio_3m_1m of 20.4—this suggests a recent inflection point rather than sustained momentum. Both securities clear all SMA thresholds and outperform SPY, but PARR's superior ratio consistency and spy-day resilience (99.04 vs lower scores elsewhere) make it the more technically stable momentum leader, while CLMT's recent acceleration warrants caution regarding whether the spike represents new momentum or mean reversion noise.
For educational and informational purposes only. Not financial advice. Past performance does not guarantee future results.
